summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements and challenges in the field, from Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI to the growing importance of AI in various industries.
insight: The increasing use of AI in industries such as healthcare, finance, and education is driving innovation, but also raises concerns about data privacy and security.
highlights: Apple files a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, potentially disrupting its IPO plans.
Google is expanding its AI Mode to let users link and interact with select apps, enhancing user experience.
The EU orders Google to open Android and Search to rivals, promoting competition in the AI market.

articles.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
articles.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief

articles.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
articles.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done

articles.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
articles.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
articles.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

articles.title: Google-backed satellites for wildfire detection launch as smoke chokes US, Canada
articles.description: The FireSat program can spot wildfires that other satellites miss.

articles.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
